Entity_Description: South Slope is a residential neighbourhood in south Burnaby, British Columbia, with a population of about 12,782 (2021). It occupies the south-facing hillside that descends toward the Fraser River, roughly 11 kilometres from downtown Vancouver and about 7 kilometres from New Westminster. The neighbourhood sits just south of Metrotown and the adjacent Maywood area, within Metro Vancouver, Canada's third-largest metropolitan economy. Its housing stock reflects Burnaby's postwar growth, mixing older single-family homes and Vancouver Specials with newer townhouse and strata developments.

Short_Description: South Slope earned its name honestly: the whole neighbourhood tilts southward, dropping from the Metrotown highlands toward the Fraser River below. That grade gives its roughly 12,782 residents river outlooks and all-day sun — and it sends every millimetre of Burnaby's heavy winter rain downhill across their properties. {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} built for exactly this terrain, serving everything from postwar family homes and Vancouver Specials to the newer strata townhomes filling in the hillside. As a {BUSINESS_TYPE} that works these sloped streets year-round, we know which problems the grade creates, where the water goes in November, and how to solve it properly the first time. Get your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} today — call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Most Burnaby neighbourhoods are named after a person or a road. South Slope is named after physics. The entire community is a south-facing hillside stepping down from the Metrotown area toward the Fraser River, and that single fact shapes almost everything about owning property here — including the {SERVICE} it needs.

Start with the water. Metro Vancouver's oceanic climate delivers the bulk of its rainfall between November and March, and on flat ground that is mostly a drainage question. On South Slope's grade, it is a gravity question. Rain that lands uphill moves downhill across driveways, retaining walls, foundations and yards on its way to the river, which is why moisture management, moss and algae growth, and slope-side wear show up here in ways a flat Richmond lot never sees. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} that treats a South Slope property like level ground is guessing; {COMPANY_NAME} plans for the slope from the first site visit.

Then there is the housing itself. South Slope grew up as part of Burnaby's postwar expansion, and its streets still carry that history: older single-family homes and classic Vancouver Specials sit alongside newer townhouse rows and strata-governed multi-family projects, part of the wave of redevelopment spreading south from Metrotown. Around 12,782 people live here, close enough to Metrotown's shops and the adjacent Maywood neighbourhood to walk, yet on quieter residential streets with Fraser River views to the south. For {SERVICE}, that mix matters — a 1950s bungalow, a 1970s Special and a 2015 strata townhome each fail differently and each demands different solutions, and strata work adds its own approval and documentation requirements that {COMPANY_NAME} handles routinely.

Location rounds out the picture. South Slope sits about 11 kilometres from downtown Vancouver and roughly 7 kilometres from New Westminster, with Maywood barely a kilometre away and central Burnaby within 4 kilometres. That puts the neighbourhood inside one of Canada's most valuable and competitive property markets, where deferred maintenance is expensive and quality work protects real equity.

{COMPANY_NAME} brings {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} of experience and a {RATING} rating to every South Slope project, backed by {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP}. Whether you are uphill near Metrotown or down toward the river, call {PHONE} for your {ESTIMATE_TYPE}.
Local_Facts:
- South Slope is a residential neighbourhood in south Burnaby, within the Metro Vancouver Regional District, British Columbia
- Population was approximately 12,782 at the 2021 Census
- The neighbourhood occupies a south-facing hillside descending toward the Fraser River
- Located about 11 km from downtown Vancouver and roughly 7 km from New Westminster
- Adjacent to the Maywood neighbourhood (about 1 km away) near Metrotown, with central Burnaby about 4 km away
- Climate is mild and oceanic, with the heaviest rainfall from November through March and rare snow at this elevation
- Housing mixes postwar single-family homes and Vancouver Specials with newer townhouse and strata developments
